dataflow.description:
### Human actors
- Developers using CLI to submit coding tasks
- Operators monitoring system via dashboard
- Repository maintainers receiving pull requests
- GitHub webhook callers triggering tasks

### External entities
- GitHub API for repository operations and issue data
- Amazon Bedrock for AI model inference (Claude Sonnet/Haiku)
- GitHub repositories containing source code to modify
- External webhook callers (CI/CD systems, issue trackers)

### Processes
- TaskApi Lambda handlers processing REST requests
- TaskOrchestrator managing task lifecycle with durable execution
- AgentCore Runtime executing isolated agent sessions
- ConcurrencyReconciler maintaining user task limits
- WebhookAuthorizer validating HMAC signatures

### Data stores

| Store | Type | High-Value (Y/N) |
|-------|------|------------------|
| TaskTable | DynamoDB | Y |
| TaskEventsTable | DynamoDB | Y |
| UserConcurrencyTable | DynamoDB | N |
| WebhookTable | DynamoDB | Y |
| RepoTable | DynamoDB | N |
| AgentMemory | Bedrock Memory | Y |
| Secrets Manager | AWS Service | Y |

### Trust boundaries

| Zone | Elements | Trust Level |
|------|----------|-------------|
| Public Internet | CLI clients, webhook callers | Untrusted |
| API Gateway | Cognito auth, WAF protection | Semi-trusted |
| VPC Private | Agent runtime, Lambda functions | Trusted |
| AWS Services | DynamoDB, Bedrock, Secrets Manager | Trusted |

### Data flows

| Flow ID | Description | Source | Target | Trust Boundaries Crossed | Assets |
|---------|-------------|--------|--------|--------------------------|--------|
| F1 | CLI task submission | Developer CLI | API Gateway | Public to Semi-trusted | Task specs, auth tokens |
| F2 | Webhook task creation | External webhook | API Gateway | Public to Semi-trusted | Task data, HMAC signature |
| F3 | Task orchestration | API Gateway | TaskOrchestrator | Semi-trusted to Trusted | Task records, user context |
| F4 | Agent execution | TaskOrchestrator | AgentCore Runtime | Trusted to Trusted | Code, prompts, GitHub tokens |
| F5 | GitHub operations | AgentCore Runtime | GitHub API | Trusted to Public | Repository data, pull requests |
| F6 | AI model inference | AgentCore Runtime | Bedrock | Trusted to Trusted | Prompts, code context |
| F7 | Memory operations | AgentCore Runtime | AgentMemory | Trusted to Trusted | Task episodes, learnings |
| F8 | State persistence | Lambda handlers | DynamoDB | Trusted to Trusted | Task state, events, metrics |
| F9 | Secret retrieval | AgentCore Runtime | Secrets Manager | Trusted to Trusted | GitHub tokens, webhook secrets |
| F10 | Dashboard queries | Operators | CloudWatch | Trusted to Trusted | Logs, metrics, traces |

assumptions.content: AgentCore Runtime provides sufficient isolation between concurrent agent sessions

metadata.value: Based on VPC isolation and MicroVM architecture described in CDK constructs

assumptions.content: GitHub API rate limits and authentication are handled appropriately by agent runtime

metadata.value: Agent performs multiple GitHub operations including cloning, branching, and PR creation

assumptions.content: DynamoDB provides adequate consistency for concurrent task state management

metadata.value: Multiple Lambda functions read/write task state with user concurrency limits enforced

assumptions.content: Webhook HMAC validation provides sufficient authentication for external task creation

metadata.value: Webhook authorizer validates signatures using secrets stored in Secrets Manager

assumptions.content: Bedrock Guardrails effectively detect and block prompt injection attacks in task descriptions

metadata.value: Task creation applies guardrails with HIGH strength for prompt attack detection

assumptions.content: Agent memory system provides value without leaking sensitive information across tasks

metadata.value: AgentMemory stores cross-task learnings and episodes for improved performance

assumptions.content: DNS firewall in observation mode provides adequate network egress monitoring

metadata.value: DNS firewall configured in observation mode for initial deployment with domain allowlists
